pageTitle: Carer & patient wellbeing sessions | Dove Cottage
heroImageAlt: A patient, carer and therapy dog in a welcoming hospice lounge
heroShortDescription: Holistic support for individuals and carers
mainIntro1: Our carer and patient support groups are open to individuals and carers living with palliative conditions. Unlike our hospice daycare provision, these sessions are for carers to accompany their loved one to the hospice and benefit themselves from some of the wellbeing activities and therapies on offer, whilst making connections with other families in similar situations.
mainIntro2: Our palliative nursing team will be on hand to offer nursing support and advice if required, and our carer’s champion will be there to provide any additional support and signposting to other relevant services that might be useful to you. A light lunch is also provided.
mainIntro3: Sessions are designed to be relaxed and flexible, with each person encouraged to take part in a way that feels right for them. Some people come for companionship and a change of scene, while others value the opportunity to try activities that support confidence, independence, and emotional wellbeing.
elementsIntro: The following areas can be included as part of our wellbeing sessions:

elementBlocks.title: Social connection
elementBlocks.intro: Our team and volunteers help create a friendly, supportive environment where guests can meet others who may understand some of what they are going through. This can include:

elementBlocks.title: Relaxation
elementBlocks.intro: Where appropriate, sessions may include activities that support relaxation and comfort. These can vary depending on availability and individual needs, but may include:

elementBlocks.title: Emotional wellbeing, advocacy, signposting and support
elementBlocks.intro: Our team can offer emotional support and help guests understand what other services may be available to them. We can also signpost to further support for family members and carers where needed. Areas we can support with include (but are not limited to) the following:

bullets.text: Family and carer support and signposting

notMedicalBody: Wellbeing sessions are not a replacement for medical care, but they can sit alongside clinical support to help people feel less alone, more informed, and more connected to their local community.
sessionTimesBody: These sessions currently take place from 10am–1pm on a Monday, with access via the hospice referral process and subject to eligibility.
relatedIntro: You can also have access to our full range of additional day services, which are listed below (click a heading to view the page).
referralIntro: To find out more, please complete the
referralOutro: and someone will be in touch to discuss in more detail.
sideFormHint: Enquire about carer and patient wellbeing sessions or ask for a referral.
